title: vernonia annamica
intro: Vernonia annamica is a plant species native to the Western Ghats in India, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and characterized by its clusters of small, yellow flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Vernonia annamica include its potential to treat fever, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its ability to enhance wound healing and protect against oxidative stress. Vernonia annamica is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmaceutical formulations.
